topics.name: Arrays
topics.summary: Learn about array traversal, rotation, searching, and sorting techniques.

problems.title: Two Sum
problems.approach: Use a hash map to store complements. For each element, check if its complement exists in the map.

problems.title: Maximum Subarray
problems.approach: Apply Kadane's Algorithm: track current sum and max sum. Reset current sum if it becomes negative.

problems.title: Merge Intervals
problems.approach: Sort intervals by start time. Merge overlapping intervals by comparing end times.

topics.name: Strings
topics.summary: Master string manipulation, pattern matching, and substring problems.

problems.title: Valid Anagram
problems.approach: Use frequency counter or sort both strings. Compare character counts or sorted versions.

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.approach: Use sliding window with a hash set. Expand window and shrink when duplicates found.

problems.title: Minimum Window Substring
problems.approach: Use two pointers and frequency map. Expand right, contract left when valid window found.

topics.name: Linked Lists
topics.summary: Understand pointer manipulation, reversal, and cycle detection in linked lists.

problems.title: Reverse Linked List
problems.approach: Use three pointers: prev, current, next. Iteratively reverse links while traversing.

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
problems.approach: Use dummy node and compare values. Link smaller node and advance pointers.

problems.title: LRU Cache
problems.approach: Combine hash map with doubly linked list. Map for O(1) access, list for LRU order.

topics.name: Trees
topics.summary: Explore tree traversals, BST operations, and tree construction problems.

problems.title: Binary Tree Inorder Traversal
problems.approach: Use recursion or stack. Visit left subtree, process node, then right subtree.

problems.title: Validate Binary Search Tree
problems.approach: Use recursion with min/max bounds. Ensure each node's value is within valid range.

problems.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
problems.approach: Use post-order DFS. Track max path through each node, update global maximum.

topics.name: Graphs
topics.summary: Learn graph traversal algorithms, shortest paths, and cycle detection.

problems.title: Number of Islands
problems.approach: Use DFS or BFS to mark connected components. Count each new island found.

problems.title: Course Schedule
problems.approach: Detect cycle in directed graph using DFS with color marking or topological sort.

problems.title: Word Ladder
problems.approach: Use BFS to find shortest transformation. Generate neighbors by changing one letter.

topics.name: Dynamic Programming
topics.summary: Solve complex problems efficiently by breaking them into overlapping subproblems.

problems.title: Climbing Stairs
problems.approach: Use Fibonacci pattern. dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]. Can optimize to O(1) space.

problems.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
problems.approach: Use DP array where dp[i] = max length ending at i. Or use binary search for O(n log n).

problems.title: Edit Distance
problems.approach: Build 2D DP table. Consider insert, delete, replace operations. Take minimum cost.

topics.name: Recursion
topics.summary: Master recursive thinking and backtracking techniques.

problems.title: Fibonacci Number
problems.approach: Base cases: F(0)=0, F(1)=1. Recursive: F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2). Use memoization.

problems.title: Permutations
problems.approach: Use backtracking. Swap elements, recurse, then backtrack. Track used elements.

problems.title: N-Queens
problems.approach: Backtrack row by row. Check column, diagonal conflicts. Place queen if safe.

topics.name: Greedy
topics.summary: Learn to make locally optimal choices to find global solutions.

problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problems.approach: Track minimum price seen. Calculate max profit at each step.

problems.title: Jump Game
problems.approach: Track farthest reachable position. Update at each step, check if goal reachable.

problems.title: Gas Station
problems.approach: Track total gas and current tank. Reset start when tank goes negative.

topics.name: Sorting & Searching
topics.summary: Master various sorting algorithms and binary search techniques.

problems.title: Binary Search
problems.approach: Use two pointers (left, right). Compare mid with target, adjust search space.

problems.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.approach: Modified binary search. Identify sorted half, check if target is in that range.

problems.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
problems.approach: Binary search on smaller array. Partition both arrays to find median position.

topics.name: Bit Manipulation
topics.summary: Understand bitwise operations and their applications in problem-solving.

problems.title: Single Number
problems.approach: Use XOR operation. XOR all numbers - duplicates cancel out, leaving single number.

problems.title: Number of 1 Bits
problems.approach: Use n & (n-1) to remove rightmost 1 bit. Count iterations until n becomes 0.

problems.title: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
problems.approach: Build Trie of binary representations. For each number, find maximum XOR path.

hackathonIdeas.title: AI-Powered Chatbot
hackathonIdeas.description: Build an intelligent chatbot using NLP and machine learning

hackathonIdeas.title: Digital Library System
hackathonIdeas.description: Create a comprehensive e-library with search and recommendation features

hackathonIdeas.title: Health Tracker App
hackathonIdeas.description: Track fitness goals, nutrition, and health metrics with data visualization

hackathonIdeas.title: Smart Home Automation
hackathonIdeas.description: IoT-based system to control and monitor home devices

hackathonIdeas.title: Collaborative Code Editor
hackathonIdeas.description: Real-time code collaboration platform with syntax highlighting

tips.title: 5-Step Problem Solving Approach
points: Understand the problem - Read carefully and identify inputs/outputs
Plan your approach - Think of algorithms and data structures
Write pseudocode - Outline your solution before coding
Implement the solution - Write clean, readable code
Test and optimize - Verify edge cases and improve time complexity

tips.title: Common DSA Patterns
points: Two Pointers - For array/string problems with sorted data
Fast & Slow Pointers - For cycle detection in linked lists
Merge Intervals - For overlapping interval problems
Top K Elements - Use heaps for finding largest/smallest elements

tips.title: Debugging Strategies
points: Use print statements to track variable values
Check boundary conditions (empty, single element, max size)
Verify your logic with pen and paper
Use debugger tools to step through code

roadmaps.title: DSA Mastery Path
roadmaps.description: From beginner to advanced competitive programmer

roadmaps.title: Web Development for Hackathons
roadmaps.description: Build full-stack projects quickly and efficiently

roadmaps.title: ML/AI for Beginners
roadmaps.description: Start your journey in machine learning and AI
